# Orokin GUI Wire

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin GUI Wire. Wire frozen contract into GUI skin. Widget → Command → core::execute → Result → render. No logic. No invented state. No invented screens.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.d `gui-wire`

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-gui-wire
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF: sha=<x> version=<y>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF + **DESIGN_SPEC**.
2. **DESIGN_SPEC missing or empty → BLOCK immediately.** Do not start.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-gui-wire`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-gui-wire`
5. Pin contract dep in `/gui/Cargo.toml` to frozen ref.

## Work

1. For each screen in DESIGN_SPEC:
   - Render Result fields to widgets.
   - Bind gestures (click, submit, etc.) to Commands.
   - Handle loading / empty / error / success states from DESIGN_SPEC.
2. For streaming Commands:
   - Use `subscribe()` / `Stream<Event>` per contract.
   - Incremental render. No buffering full stream before paint.
3. Client-side state rule: **only what derives from Result/Event**. No invented state.
4. Tests:
   - E2E covering DESIGN_SPEC happy path.
   - E2E covering one error path.
5. A11y: keyboard reachable, labels present.
6. Verify screens render via platform QA tool per user pref (khora for web, loki for desktop, qorvex for ios).
7. Open PR on `/gui`. Title: `gui: <feature>`.

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "VERIFY: <tool> <screens verified>"
limbo note $TASK "TESTS: e2e happy + error pass"
limbo note $TASK "COMMIT: <sha> — <one-line summary>"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-gui-wire --outcome "gui wired"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: gui PR <url>
```

## Block

Any of:
- DESIGN_SPEC missing, incomplete, or contradicts SPEC acceptance
- Result needs transformation beyond formatting (contract wrong)
- Gesture has no matching Command (contract wrong)
- asked to touch core, contract, or other skin

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: gui
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-gui-wire
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Touch only `/gui`.
- Pin contract to FROZEN_CONTRACT_REF.
- Every gesture maps to a Command (or Subscribe if declared).
- Every screen region maps to a Result / Event shape.
- No client-side state not derivable from Result/Event.
- No invented screens. DESIGN_SPEC is law.
- A11y baseline: keyboard + labels.
- Kill dev server/app processes after verify (`pkill` per user pref).
- Stop on ambiguity. Never invent.

# Orokin Integrator

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin Integrator. Final gate. Merge skin PRs into umbrella. Run full test matrix. Verify invariants. Open umbrella PR for human merge.

Mechanical conflicts (Cargo.lock, format, auto-gen) you fix. Logic conflicts you reject.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.f `integrator` + §4 INVARIANTS

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-integrator
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + all sibling PR urls from notes.
2. `limbo tree $FEATURE_ROOT` — list sibling roles + statuses.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-integrator`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-integrator`

## Work

### 1. Gather PRs

Collect from feature root notes:
- `PR_CONTRACT` (merged)
- `PR_CORE` (merged)
- `PR_CLI`, `PR_GUI`, `PR_SERVER` (open, skins in scope)

If any in-scope skin PR missing → BLOCK.

### 2. Check out + build

Check out each skin PR branch onto a fresh integration branch. Build workspace:

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
```

### 3. Resolve conflicts

**Mechanical — you fix:**
- Cargo.lock regen
- import ordering / formatter output
- CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` ordering
- auto-generated schema files

**Logic — you reject:**
- two PRs editing same function body
- semantic differences in shared helpers
- dep version mismatches

Logic conflict found → REJECTED block (see below). Do NOT fix.

### 4. Run full test matrix

```
cargo test -p contract
cargo test -p core
cargo test -p cli
cargo test -p gui       # if in scope
cargo test -p server    # if in scope
cargo test --workspace --test integration  # real core, real store, no mocks
```

All must pass. Any failure → reject the responsible PR.

### 5. Verify SPEC acceptance criteria

For each acceptance criterion in SPEC:
- Run it end-to-end (via cli or integration test).
- Record one-line confirmation in umbrella PR description.

Any unmet → reject offending layer's PR.

### 6. Verify §4 invariants

Tick every box in protocol §4:

```
[ ] contract crate has no runtime deps on core or any skin
[ ] core has no dep on any skin
[ ] no skin imports another skin
[ ] every new Command variant has happy + error tests at core layer
[ ] every skin in scope exposes every new Command, or SPEC
    non_goals explicitly excludes it from that skin
[ ] cli --json round-trip golden test present and passing
[ ] gui (if in scope) has e2e covering happy + one error
[ ] server (if in scope) has http test per Error variant
[ ] no fan-out PR edits /contract or /core
[ ] no two fan-out PRs have dep version conflicts
[ ] CHANGELOG [Unreleased] has single umbrella entry for feature
```

Any unchecked → reject or fix mechanically if possible.

### 7. Open umbrella PR

Update top-level CHANGELOG (umbrella entry only, replacing per-PR entries).

```bash
gh pr create --title "feat: <feature>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
<one-line intent>

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] <criterion 1>
- [x] <criterion 2>
- ...

## Invariants
- [x] <§4 items>

## Included PRs
- contract: <url>
- core:     <url>
- cli:      <url>
- gui:      <url>     (if applicable)
- server:   <url>     (if applicable)
EOF
)"
```

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "UMBRELLA_PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "INVARIANTS: all ticked"
limbo note $TASK "ACCEPTANCE: all met"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-integrator --outcome "umbrella ready for human merge"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: umbrella PR <url> ready for merge
```

## Reject (logic conflict or test fail)

```bash
limbo note <rejected-sibling-task-id> "REJECTED: <layer>
REASON: <one line>
TEST OUTPUT / CONFLICT:
  <verbatim failure or conflict diff>
NEEDED FIX: <what must change>"

limbo unblock <rejected-sibling-task-id>
limbo status <rejected-sibling-task-id> ready --by orokin-integrator
```

Then block self pending re-work:

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "blocked-on: <sibling> rework" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: rejected <layer>: <reason>
BLOCK_REASON: awaiting <sibling> rework
```

Orchestrator reopens rejected sibling next tick.

## Block (non-reject)

Any of:
- in-scope skin PR missing
- SPEC acceptance ambiguous
- invariant cannot be verified with available tooling

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: integrator
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Do NOT edit fan-out PR logic. Mechanical fixes only.
- Integration tests hit skin → real core → real store. No mocks.
- Every acceptance criterion confirmed in umbrella PR.
- Every §4 invariant ticked or rejected.
- Umbrella PR title: `feat: <feature>` always.
- CHANGELOG: single umbrella entry. Remove per-PR entries.
- Skip failing test → forbidden.
- Merge without human approval → forbidden.
- Rejection uses REJECTED format verbatim.

# PM task lifecycle

```
captured --> triage ─┬─ (leaf)  --> refined --> planned --> ready --> in-progress --> in-review --> done
                     │                                         ^              |
                     │                                         |   rollback   |
                     │                                         +--------------+
                     │
                     └─ (split) --> children created via `limbo add --parent`,
                                    parent dependency-blocked until children done,
                                    orchestrator cleanup walk promotes parent to done
```

Each stage tracked by limbo. PM enforces workflow. Limbo = pure task store.

**Every leaf task goes through every stage. No skip. No exception for leaves.**
**Parents (tasks that split during triage) blocked by children. Orchestrator cleanup walk promotes directly `captured` → `done` when all children done.**

# Helios activation

Activation: 1) Verify helios on PATH with `which helios`. 2) Determine project dir. 3) If .helios/index.db exists → `helios update` (incremental); else → `helios init`. 4) If init reports 0 files, fall back to Glob/Grep. 5) Use helios symbols/deps/summary to answer questions.

caveat: "step 2–5 fail → kill direct"
prereq:
  - khora installed

# Standard khora workflow

1. `SESSION=$(khora --format json launch | jq -r .id)`
2. `khora navigate "$SESSION" "https://your-app.com"`
3. `khora wait-for "$SESSION" "<selector>"`
4. `khora screenshot "$SESSION" -o /tmp/screenshot.png`
5. `khora text` / `attribute` / `click` / `type` as needed
6. `khora kill "$SESSION"`

If any step 2–5 fails, jump directly to kill.

# Window interaction pattern

1. `loki launch <bundle-id>`.
2. `loki wait-window --bundle-id <id>`.
3. `WINDOW=$(loki windows --bundle-id <id> -f json | jq -r '.[0].window_id')`.
4. Use `$WINDOW` in subsequent commands.

# App exploration phases

Exploration phases in order:

1. Initial Survey — screenshot, screen-info, record elements/nav structure.
2. Screen Discovery — breadth-first, tap each exit, map destinations, handle modals, scroll for more.
3. Interaction Testing — text fields, toggles, pickers, search.
4. Flow Identification — onboarding, auth, primary tasks, error states.
5. Output Generation — write artifacts to `/tmp/app-explorer/<bundle-id>/`.

- Never capture one-off situational choices

# Lotus anti-rationalization principle

Lotus retros must contain at least one gate-0 spec failure attributable to lotus.

If a draft retro has zero lotus-attributed failures and ten tooling-attributed failures, the retro is incomplete. Re-dispatch devil with the prompt: "find the lotus failure I missed."

Externalizing every failure to tools or downstream agents is the self-exculpation pattern (devil retro atom 019de408-8758). The org learns to fix tools and never decisions. Forbidden.

## Application

- Any retro authored with `--tags lotus,retro` must pass this check before being slugged or marked verified.
- The `lotus-gate-0-selfcheck` procedure (slug) feeds this principle by creating a per-mission lotus-failure record up-front.
- Devil's mandate now includes auditing retros against this principle.

# Workspace-member isolation as a dep-cap firewall

When adding a heavy experimental dep stack (lance, datafusion, arrow, tantivy) to an existing Rust project that has tight dep-surface budget, create a NEW workspace member crate (e.g. `simaris-m3-lance/`) instead of feature-gating in the main crate.

Why: the main `simaris` crate stays at its existing 8-dep surface. The experimental crate carries the +115 transitive deps. `cargo build` on the main crate is still fast; `cargo build -p simaris-m3-lance` is the slow one and only the prototype owner pays that cost. Production binaries (`simaris`, `simaris-server`) don't pick up any of it.

Workspace member crates also let you set `publish = false` and a separate `name` so it never accidentally ships to crates.io even if someone runs `cargo publish --workspace`.

Trade-off: doesn't help if the prototype must call back into the parent crate's internals — you'd need to expose those as a library interface or duplicate. For pure migration scripts that only read the parent's data file (sqlite, etc.), the trade-off is firmly net-positive.

Surfaced from M3.2 simaris LanceDB prototype, branch `m3/lancedb`.

# Frontmatter migration — friction log

- **F1 (fixed P3a.1)**: rewrite cancel path wrote skeleton. Fixed via buffer-init vs buffer-final compare (commit `04c3aab`).
- **F2 (open)**: empty-only frontmatter still commits on save-without-fill. Low-risk currently (F1 covers the common cancel path). Capture + patch if dogfood surfaces pain.
- **F3 (open)**: no diff preview / TTY confirm before DB write. Dogfood on hot units risky without backup. Consider `--diff` mode or interactive prompt if loss happens.
- **F4 (fixed P3a.2)**: `strip_header_comments` stopped at first blank line after `#` header, leaving `\n` prefix. `has_frontmatter` requires `---` at byte 0 → fresh rewrites fell through to raw-YAML render + still surfaced as unstructured. Fixed by consuming separator blank + latching `in_header=false` once seen (commit `1ff3dbf`).
- **F5 (deferred)**: body-size warn threshold (2048B) trips on legitimately long aspects like `orokin-server-wire` (2992B). Consider per-type thresholds or loosen for aspects specifically — aspects carry role/boot/work/block procedure blocks and run long by design.
- **F6 (retracted)**: earlier concern that list-key rendering would be noisy was wrong. `render_markdown` already switches to bullets for >3 items or mixed types — verified on `researcher` (6 refs → bullets) and `tech-lead` (16 refs → bullets). Comma-join only fires on ≤3 scalars.
- **F7 (observation)**: atom-index aspects (split-from-monolith) map cleanly to `refs: [sub-atom-slug, ...]`. The `refs` field becomes load-bearing — future tooling (navigation, orchestrator loading) can follow the list. Suggests P5 (`schema_json` column) value for these queries.
- **F8 (observation)**: pre-split monoliths (e.g. `019d97cf` tech-lead, `019d97ce` researcher) still exist as aspect units alongside their split families. Should be marked `outdated` via `mark` command or linked with `supersedes` edges (some already have this). Out of scope for migration but worth a cleanup pass.
- **F9 (observation)**: umbrella plan `frontmatter-migration` itself hit hard threshold (8202B > 8192B default). `--force` override used, then split per `split-ruleset` into this unit + ship-log + dogfood-log + phases + dogfood-procedure. Dogfoods the decomposition rules.
- **F10 (fixed `25746ff`)**: `UserPromptSubmit` hook showed `---` as the headline for structured units. Root cause: `derive_headline` returned the first non-empty line of `content`, which is the frontmatter fence. Fix: parse frontmatter off first; use body's first line; fall back to `role`/`scope`/`trigger`/`context`/`tension` scalar if body empty. Lands 7 unit tests.
- **F11 (observation, P3b)**: `--suggest` non-determinism — dry-run draft and real-run draft differ across LLM calls. On `verifier` rewrite: dry-run filled `dispatches_to: [verifier-khora, verifier-loki, verifier-qorvex]` (treating QA tools as delegates); real run produced `dispatches_to: []` and added a 6th flow step to `handles_directly`. Both reasonable; second arguably more correct (khora/loki/qorvex are tools, not roles). User flow `dry-run → eyeball → accept` can surprise. Mitigations: (a) cache dry-run output to temp file, real run consumes cached draft instead of re-calling claude; (b) document expectation; (c) lower temperature in `build_prompt`. Defer until pain surfaces in bulk dogfood.
- **F12 (observation, P3b batch-1)**: empty `refs: []` in YAML renders as `**refs:** ` (bare key + trailing space, no value). Surfaced on `vox-cli`, `verify-flag-before-trust`, `mirage-dag-toggle` — three of five batch-1 units chose `refs: []`. Visual artifact, not breakage. Fix candidates: (a) `render_markdown` skips keys with empty list values; (b) `--suggest` prompt instructs LLM to omit `refs` rather than emit `[]`. Low priority — not user-facing bug, just untidy `simaris show` output.
- **F13 (observation, P3b batch-1)**: F11 non-determinism confirmed cross-type, mild magnitude this batch. Every batch-1 unit (aspect/procedure/principle/lesson/fact) showed real-vs-dry-run drift on ≥1 field — examples: `worker` `dispatches_to` shape (list → inline string), `vox-cli` `prereq` shape (list → string), `verify-flag-before-trust` body header added in real-run. None changed semantic content; all judgment-call drift the user-flow review caught implicitly. Confirms F11 is a cross-type property of `--suggest`, not aspect-specific. Mitigations from F11 still apply; bulk-dogfood pain remains tolerable so far.
- **F14 (observation, batch-2)**: `scan --unstructured` surfaces units that already have incoming `supersedes` edges — effectively already-obsolete units queued for rewrite. Hit during batch-2 candidate selection: 2 of initial 5 picks (`019d7876-9f37` two-async-patterns, `019d73ee-2c31` aspects-self-sufficient) had an incoming supersedes edge, making rewrite pointless. Fix candidates: (a) add `--exclude-superseded` filter flag; (b) make exclude-superseded default + add `--include-superseded` opt-in. (b) preferred — supersedes is the clear signal the unit is already obsolete.
- **F15 (observation, batch-2)**: `--suggest` LLM-inferred refs live in frontmatter as UUIDs but do NOT auto-create `related_to` graph edges. Hit on `019d8dba` (limbo-status lesson) — LLM correctly inferred `refs: [019da0a4-1a0f-...]` pointing to `verify-flag-before-trust` principle; the ref is visible in `simaris show` but `ask`/graph-expansion won't traverse it. Fix candidates: (a) write-path hook that parses `refs:` on save, validates each is an existing UUID/slug, inserts `related_to` edge (idempotent); (b) document frontmatter-refs as non-edge cross-references and require explicit `simaris link` for graph semantics. (a) preferred — LLM inference is high-signal graph enrichment and losing it is waste.
- **F11 (update, batch-2)**: non-determinism now confirmed WITHIN single unit across retries — not only across types. Two consecutive `--dry-run` calls on `lotus` aspect produced different `handles_directly` scalar phrasings ('chat + rubber-duck + weigh options' vs 'chat + rubber-duck'). Strengthens mitigation (a): **cache dry-run output to temp file, real run consumes cached draft** — without this, the user-flow `dry-run → eyeball → accept` is technically lying because accept re-calls the LLM. Elevate (a) from deferred to queued; batch-3+ will feel this more on hot units.
- **F16 (observation, batch-3+4)**: LLM emits self-reference in `refs:` — the unit's own UUID appears in its own refs list. Hit on `019dbba2-74e8` (researcher scout aspect): refs included its own ID alongside sibling sub-aspects. F15 hook correctly skips self-edges, but the YAML scalar still contains the self-ref token. Low priority — cosmetic; consider stripping at prompt time.
- **F17 (mitigated, batch-3+4)**: sonnet hallucinates `refs:` UUIDs from corpus-memory on bodies that have no UUIDs to anchor on. Hit during batch-3+4 unattended bulk: aspects without sibling UUIDs in body received refs lists of 4-24 plausible-related UUIDs (researcher siblings, code-review procedures, mirage units). DETERMINISTIC across runs — same prompt → same UUIDs in same order. Source: Claude's training/memory of the simaris corpus across session transcripts (not the UserPromptSubmit hook — `--settings '{"hooks":{}}'` neutralizes that and contamination still appears). Mitigations applied (commit `0e5e3c5`): (a) `call_claude` passes `--settings '{"hooks":{}}'` to disable parent hook injection (defensive even if not the primary source). (b) prompt constraint added: `refs:` MUST be body-derived; default `[]`. Haiku honors cleanly; sonnet partial-honors and still hallucinates on UUID-less aspect bodies. (c) F15 graph-edge hook gracefully skips unresolvable refs (e.g. truncated 8-char UUIDs from body), so no broken edges land. Net: hallucinated edges that DO land are real-and-related (verified by spot-check), not garbage.
- **F16/F17 nuance**: the deterministic + plausible nature of sonnet's contamination is not strictly noise — it's auto-curation of related-unit edges from Claude's memory. Whether this is signal or spam is a values call. v1 leans cautious (constraint + hook fix). If batch-5+ shows the auto-curated edges are net useful for `ask`-expansion, consider reverting the constraint and embracing it as a feature.
- **F18 (observation, batch-5)**: `scan --unstructured` over-counts unschemaed types. `idea` and `preference` carry no frontmatter schema (per `rewrite.rs:262`), so `--suggest` emits body-only and `SIMARIS_EDITOR=true` saves it without a `---` fence. Unit logs OK in driver but stays flagged in `scan --unstructured`. Hit on 4 of 5 stuck units in batch-5 (`019d73a1` aspect-arch, `019db79f` simaris-LOD, `019db7be` body-size-enforcement, `019daabf` mirage-admin-CLI — all type=`idea`). Fix candidates: (a) `scan --unstructured` filter excludes `idea`/`preference` by default + opt-in flag; (b) define a minimal schema for `idea`/`preference` (e.g. `tags: list`, `refs: list`); (c) drop `idea`/`preference` from candidate-picking heuristic in bulk-rewrite driver. (a) preferred — matches existing `scan --include-superseded` precedent and respects schema-less types as feature, not gap.

- **F19 (observation, batch-14)**: bulk-rewrite driver doesn't pass `--force`/`--flow` to `simaris rewrite --suggest`, so any LLM-drafted body exceeding 8192B hard threshold rejects with non-zero exit and the unit stays unstructured. Hit 1/50 in batch-14 (`019db109` — 11293B draft, retried manually with `--force`, accepted at 7415B post-warn). Pattern: large procedure/aspect bodies expand under LLM rewrite (skeleton + preserved body + frontmatter overhead). Fix candidates: (a) driver retries failed ids with `--force` automatically; (b) `rewrite --suggest` gains an opt-in `--auto-flow-on-overflow` flag; (c) accept hard threshold and surface to user — current state (manual retry path is functional but adds friction at scale). (a) preferred for unattended bulk; threshold review out of scope.

- **F20 (observation, batch-16)**: LLM occasionally emits malformed YAML in the frontmatter block (e.g. `malformed frontmatter YAML: mapping values are not allowed in this context at line 5 column 63`). Fallback path engages (skeleton + body), unit becomes structured at minimum-viable level. Hit 1/50 in batch-16 (`019dbb62-b08e`). Likely cause: LLM emitting unquoted `:`-containing values or list items inside scalar fields. No fix needed — fallback cleanly absorbs. If rate climbs past ~5%, consider: (a) prompt nudge "quote all values containing colons"; (b) post-LLM YAML validate + single retry before fallback.

# Three challenge questions for Lotus — Banshee gate-0 assumptions

## Q1: Task identity crisis
Gate-0 assumes the UI can meaningfully label "which agent is doing what."
Reality: task text is NOT in the JSONL stream. It lives only in `/tmp/launchagent-<sid>.pretty`
header lines (written by launchagent-tmux). Agents spawned via other wrappers (direct `claude -p`,
launchagent-cmux, future tools) produce no `.pretty` companion.

Attack: If Banshee falls back to showing raw SIDs, the phone UI is not "watch agents" — 
it's "watch UUIDs." Does Lotus accept "sid-only labels" as MVP-viable, or must Banshee
parse `.pretty` files as a first-class data source? Either answer changes the data model.

## Q2: No reliable "agent is active now" signal
Gate-0 scope: "list active + recent agents." Active detection options in Rust/Banshee:
- Check for absence of a `result` event in JSONL (works, but only after file is read fully).
- Cross-reference tmux sessions (bash+macOS-only, not viable for a Rust service).
- Poll file mtime (recent = probably active, but wrong for slow agents or disk delays).

Attack: On phone via Tailscale, Banshee has no out-of-band "alive" signal per agent.
A completed agent looks identical to a stalled one mid-stream. If the "active" badge is
unreliable, Simon will stop trusting the UI — and the kill criterion fires. What is the
accepted false-positive rate for "active" states, and does Lotus sanction mtime-heuristic
as sufficient?

## Q3: 7-day retention vs 7-day kill criterion conflict
launchagent-tmux prunes `.jsonl` files older than 7 days (hardcoded `find -mtime +7`).
Gate-0 kill criterion: Simon opens Banshee URL ≥3 times within 7 days of MVP shipping.

Attack: If Simon ships MVP on day 0 and is busy for 3 days, then opens Banshee on days
4, 5, 6 — he sees agents from days 0-3 fading out of retention. By day 7, "recent agents"
may be empty. The retention window and the evaluation window are the same length, creating
a race. Does Lotus intend Banshee to be useful for historical review, or only "right now"
monitoring? If the former, the 7-day prune in launchagent-tmux must be extended or Banshee
must persist its own index. If the latter, the gate-0 falsifier list should explicitly state
"stale list is not a bug."

# Banshee wrong-problem critique — devil gate-2.5

## Steelman: `launchagent serve` subcommand instead of Banshee

`019de4a4-a908` overlap check dismissed extending existing tools but didn't steelman extending launchagent-tmux itself.

`launchagent log <sid>` already parses `.jsonl` and pretty-prints it. Add `launchagent serve [--bind :PORT]`: a 150-line axum handler that merges all active agent pretty-streams into a single SSE endpoint, served as `text/plain`. Phone opens `http://banshee:PORT/` → browser renders a live scrolling text feed of all agent output. Zero HTML. Zero JS. Zero tree-rendering. Zero schema-parsing concern. Handles the `.pretty` label problem automatically (launchagent already knows the format). Ship in 1 day. Same brew distribution. Genuinely solves "can't watch from phone."

## Counter

The steelman delivers ~30% of Banshee's value and fails on the core multi-agent oversight case. When Simon has 4 agents running concurrently (the primary motivation), a merged text stream is unnavigable — you can't jump to a specific agent, you can't collapse tool call noise, you can't tell which agent produced which line. The agent-list-view + per-agent-timeline IS the product. Without it, the phone UX degrades to "tail -f /dev/chaos."

The steelman also hits the same infra primitives (axum, file-watching, SSE bridge from `019de4a3-ee10`) — the scope savings are smaller than they appear. The real saved cost is only the frontend.

**Verdict:** Banshee is the right answer IF the structured timeline is genuinely better than a text dump. If the MVP frontend ends up as a `<pre>` block with raw event JSON, the steelman wins and Banshee should be killed and replaced with the 150-line version. Lotus should define what "structured" means before eng builds any frontend.

# Lotus failures — vox Telegram bridge gate 2.5

Three failures recorded after devil critique 2026-05-01. Per lotus-anti-rationalization principle.

## F1 — Assumed token-stream schema without verifying
Pre-pdm direction designed debounce engine, rate-limit batching, flush-on-tool_use boundary — all predicated on token-level streaming. Did not run `jq .type` on a real /tmp/launchagent-*.jsonl before deciding.

Falsifier that should have been at gate 0: "I have read a real launchagent jsonl and confirmed event granularity (token vs content-block vs turn)."

Correction: schema verified inline as content-block-atomic (atom 019de539). Debounce engine dropped from scope.

## F2 — Chose "busy reject" knowing user has ADHD
CLAUDE.md: "Simon has ADHD." Devil M3 correctly flagged "busy, try again" as the exact loop-and-retry pattern that fails ADHD users. Lotus chose serial concurrency with rejection for implementation simplicity, not user fit.

Correction: serial concurrency stands, but rejection UX changes. New: FIFO queue with explicit position reply ("queued: 1 ahead, ETA from prior session avg"). Also auto-acks every received msg with "📥 received" so Simon never wonders if it landed.

## F3 — Named falsifier "no thread memory" then accepted scope that fires it
Gate-0 falsifier list explicitly named: "Telegram thread context unusable when each msg = new session — Simon types follow-ups, lotus has no memory of prior thread." Then chose A (one-shot) with zero compensating mechanism beyond "simaris re-prime loads identity."

Devil M4 correct: simaris re-prime ≠ thread context.

Correction options:
- (a) Inject last-N Telegram msgs from same thread into the lotus prompt (cheap, partial)
- (b) Accept the trade and tighten kill-criterion to detect when this falsifier fires
- (c) Defer to v2 — ship without and watch for the failure

Decision: (a). On thread reply (Telegram message has reply_to_message_id), pull thread history from Telegram, append as `--- prior thread ---\n<N msgs>\n--- current ---` prefix to the new task. ~10 lines. Closes the falsifier.

## F5 — Kill-criterion unmeasurable
Devil M5 correct. /tmp wipes on reboot, no count mechanism. Cannot evaluate "≥3 completed sessions in 14 days."

Correction: vox listen writes a session log line per top-level msg to `~/.local/state/vox/bridge-sessions.log` (or platform equivalent). Format: `<iso8601>\t<sid>\t<status:queued|started|completed|failed>\t<task-headline>`. Survives reboot. 14-day audit = `awk` or `jq` over this file.

## Pattern (cumulative)
F1, F2, F3 share a root: lotus made implementation-convenient choices and labeled them decisions. F1 = skipped a verification step. F2 = ignored a user-context constraint. F3 = wrote a falsifier and didn't engineer against it.

The anti-rationalization principle held. Devil caught all three. Recording per principle so the next gate-0 reads against this list.

# Lotus failure — treated filed atom as message to running agent (2026-05-03)

## What happened
Simaris agent returned a charter proposal asking "confirm or push back". Agent then EXITED. Lotus filed the confirmation as `lotus-reply-simaris-charter-2026-05-03` and treated that as having communicated. Result: standstill. The reply was passive storage; no agent was reading it. User had to tell lotus the company was at standstill.

## Root cause
Confused two patterns:
- ATOMS are pull-mode (agent reads on next session/launch)
- RUNNING AGENTS need either active dispatch OR a reply-channel (nova reply-slug)

Filed atom did neither. It will be read next time simaris is launched, but the launch never happened automatically.

## Pattern
Subagent dispatch via `launchagent` is one-shot. Agent runs → outputs → exits. There is no "in-flight conversation." Replies go through:
- `--reply-slug <slug>` to post into nova inbox (which agent presumably checks), OR
- next `launchagent` invocation that includes the reply context

## Correction (immediate)
- After ANY synchronous launchagent run that ends with the agent asking lotus a question: lotus must EITHER re-launch with the answer embedded OR post via nova reply-slug. Filing a "reply atom" does neither.
- For long-running programs (like the knowledge program), prefer dispatching with explicit "execute, don't ask" framing so the agent makes the call autonomously.

## Devil note
Twice now in one session: lotus has filed atoms believing they were communications. First time was the "no confirmation needed" reply. The pattern suggests lotus's mental model treats simaris-the-store as an async message bus, but it isn't — there are no listeners.

## Procedure update needed
Add a procedure: "After launchagent that ends with question to lotus → re-launch or use reply-slug. Never treat a filed atom as a sent message."

# tooling lesson — git worktree for parallel branch agents

## Trigger
Two simaris deputy agents dispatched in parallel on different branches (m3/sqlite-vec + m3/lancedb). Single working tree shared between branches creates working-tree pollution: one agent's uncommitted Cargo.toml + untracked `simaris-m3-lance/` workspace member followed me when I checked out my branch. Risk: I'd accidentally commit their files, OR their concurrent edits would race my reads.

## Fix
`git worktree add ~/claudehub/simaris-m3-vec m3/sqlite-vec` — separate physical directory backed by the same .git, fully isolated working tree. Each parallel agent gets their own checkout. No more cross-contamination.

## When to apply
Any time `launchagent` dispatches >1 agent that will mutate the working tree of the same repo on different branches. Cheap (just an extra dir), saves the merge/conflict tax.

## Related
Cargo workspace `members = [...]` arrays are particularly hazardous — one agent adding a workspace member modifies Cargo.toml on the shared root in a way that breaks the other agent's `cargo build` (workspace member dir doesn't exist on their branch). Worktree isolation neutralizes this entirely.
